lines.line: # Introduction and Background of Brad Ritchie

> The interview begins with Hannah Laurie Hine, the state historian for the Idaho State Historical Society, introducing herself and the purpose of the oral history project. The speaker, Brad Ritchie, is asked to state his name and spell it out. He then discusses his background, including his military service in the US Air Force and the National Guard, and his journey from Louisville, Kentucky to his current role in Idaho. The speaker reflects on his career path, his motivations for joining the military, and his transition to civilian life, including his time as a welder and his eventual move to Idaho.

metadata.title: Introduction and Background of Brad Ritchie
metadata.synopsis: The interview begins with Hannah Laurie Hine, the state historian for the Idaho State Historical Society, introducing herself and the purpose of the oral history project. The speaker, Brad Ritchie, is asked to state his name and spell it out. He then discusses his background, including his military service in the US Air Force and the National Guard, and his journey from Louisville, Kentucky to his current role in Idaho. The speaker reflects on his career path, his motivations for joining the military, and his transition to civilian life, including his time as a welder and his eventual move to Idaho.

lines.line: # Transition to Emergency Management Role

> The speaker describes his transition from the military to his current role as the director of the Office of Emergency Management. He recounts the circumstances that led to his appointment, including the sudden departure of the previous director and the request from state leadership to step into the role. The speaker also touches on his interactions with Governor Otter and the challenges he faced early in his tenure, such as dealing with wildfires and other disasters in Idaho.

metadata.title: Transition to Emergency Management Role

He recounts the circumstances that led to his appointment, including the sudden departure of the previous director and the request from state leadership to step into the role. The speaker also touches on his interactions with Governor Otter and the challenges he faced early in his tenure, such as dealing with wildfires and other disasters in Idaho.

lines.line: # Reflections on Pre-Covid Emergency Management

> The speaker reflects on memorable moments in his position before the Covid-19 pandemic, highlighting the importance of leadership and the ability to respond to various disasters across the state. He shares anecdotes about working with Governor Otter and Governor Little, emphasizing their intelligence, leadership qualities, and the impact they had on the state's emergency management strategies.

metadata.title: Reflections on Pre-Covid Emergency Management

He shares anecdotes about working with Governor Otter and Governor Little, emphasizing their intelligence, leadership qualities, and the impact they had on the state's emergency management strategies.

lines.line: # Early Covid-19 Response

> The speaker discusses the early response to Covid-19, detailing how his previous experience with pandemic exercises informed the state's preparedness. He recounts the initial steps taken by the Idaho Office of Emergency Management, including monitoring the situation in Washington state and coordinating with other states. The speaker also describes the logistical challenges of repatriating Idahoans from cruise ships and the steps taken to ensure their safe return.

metadata.title: Early Covid-19 Response
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses the early response to Covid-19, detailing how his previous experience with pandemic exercises informed the state's preparedness. He recounts the initial steps taken by the Idaho Office of Emergency Management, including monitoring the situation in Washington state and coordinating with other states. The speaker also describes the logistical challenges of repatriating Idahoans from cruise ships and the steps taken to ensure their safe return.

lines.line: # Stay-at-Home Order and Management Response

> The speaker discusses the stay-at-home order issued by Governor Little and the management response that followed. He explains the rationale behind the order, based on recommendations from the Covid-19 working group and the lack of a vaccine at the time. The speaker also details the planning for continuity of government operations and the challenges of managing the state's response to the pandemic.

metadata.title: Stay-at-Home Order and Management Response
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses the stay-at-home order issued by Governor Little and the management response that followed. He explains the rationale behind the order, based on recommendations from the Covid-19 working group and the lack of a vaccine at the time. The speaker also details the planning for continuity of government operations and the challenges of managing the state's response to the pandemic.

lines.line: # Social Distancing and Preventative Measures

> The conversation turns to the topic of social distancing and other preventative measures. The speaker addresses the concerns raised by some legislators about the emergency declaration and its implications. He clarifies the importance of maintaining the declaration for the continuity of state and federal assistance, as well as the ongoing support to communities affected by the pandemic.

metadata.title: Social Distancing and Preventative Measures
metadata.synopsis: The conversation turns to the topic of social distancing and other preventative measures. The speaker addresses the concerns raised by some legislators about the emergency declaration and its implications. He clarifies the importance of maintaining the declaration for the continuity of state and federal assistance, as well as the ongoing support to communities affected by the pandemic.

lines.line: # Collaboration with Nez Perce Tribe

> The speaker discusses the collaboration between his office and the Nez Perce tribe, as well as other tribes during the pandemic. He emphasizes the importance of the relationship with the tribes and the efforts made to provide them with personal protective equipment and other resources. The speaker also highlights the logistical efforts to deliver supplies to remote tribal locations.

metadata.title: Collaboration with Nez Perce Tribe

He emphasizes the importance of the relationship with the tribes and the efforts made to provide them with personal protective equipment and other resources. The speaker also highlights the logistical efforts to deliver supplies to remote tribal locations.

lines.line: # Personal Reflection on Serving in the Working Group

> The speaker reflects on his personal experience serving on the Covid-19 working group, the vaccination group, and the crisis standards of care group. He emphasizes the importance of understanding the situation to provide effective support to Idaho's population and the value of collaboration among various stakeholders. The speaker expresses pride in the resilience of Idahoans and the state's ability to transition back to normalcy.

metadata.title: Personal Reflection on Serving in the Working Group
metadata.synopsis: The speaker reflects on his personal experience serving on the Covid-19 working group, the vaccination group, and the crisis standards of care group. He emphasizes the importance of understanding the situation to provide effective support to Idaho's population and the value of collaboration among various stakeholders. The speaker expresses pride in the resilience of Idahoans and the state's ability to transition back to normalcy.

lines.line: # National Guard Activation and Workforce Concerns

> The speaker talks about the activation of the National Guard to assist in healthcare facilities and the Idaho Department of Corrections. He discusses the precautions taken to protect Guardsmen from contracting Covid and the strategic use of the Guard to minimize impact on their federal mission. The speaker also mentions the innovative solutions developed by healthcare providers to address staffing shortages.

metadata.title: National Guard Activation and Workforce Concerns
metadata.synopsis: The speaker talks about the activation of the National Guard to assist in healthcare facilities and the Idaho Department of Corrections. He discusses the precautions taken to protect Guardsmen from contracting Covid and the strategic use of the Guard to minimize impact on their federal mission. The speaker also mentions the innovative solutions developed by healthcare providers to address staffing shortages.

lines.line: # After Action Report and Collaboration with Health and Welfare

> The speaker discusses the After Action Report, which identified strengths and areas for improvement in the state's pandemic response. He details the collaboration between the Office of Emergency Management and the Department of Health and Welfare, highlighting the joint efforts in inventory management, distribution of supplies, and communication with healthcare providers. The speaker also touches on the challenges of not having a warehouse and the solutions found to manage resources effectively.

metadata.title: After Action Report and Collaboration with Health and Welfare
metadata.synopsis: The speaker discusses the After Action Report, which identified strengths and areas for improvement in the state's pandemic response. He details the collaboration between the Office of Emergency Management and the Department of Health and Welfare, highlighting the joint efforts in inventory management, distribution of supplies, and communication with healthcare providers. The speaker also touches on the challenges of not having a warehouse and the solutions found to manage resources effectively.

lines.line: # Overall Reflection on Pandemic Response

> In the final part of the interview, the speaker provides an overall reflection on the state's response to the pandemic. He acknowledges that the nation and state were not as prepared as they could have been for a pandemic of this scale. However, he believes that the foundation set during this pandemic will aid future generations in handling similar crises. The speaker hopes that the lessons learned and the archives kept will be valuable resources for future emergency management efforts.

story.title: COVID-19 Oral History with Brad Richy
story.description: The interview with Brad Richy, the director of the Idaho Office of Emergency Management, covered his extensive background in the military and his transition to emergency management. He reflected on the early actions and challenges during the COVID-19 pandemic, emphasizing the collaboration with various agencies, including the Idaho Department of Health and Welfare, to manage resources and logistics effectively. Richy highlighted the importance of the working group formed under Governor Little, which facilitated decision-making and coordination. He expressed pride in Idaho's response, noting that despite initial unpreparedness, the state managed the situation well compared to others. Looking forward, he hopes that the experiences and lessons from the pandemic can serve as a foundation for future public health challenges.
